III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 We all know that sleep is very important to our health. When we can’t sleep well or have enough sleep, we would live shorter and have all kinds of diseases, 21 high blood pressure and heart problems. How can we sleep better at night? There are many sleep tips. First, stick to a sleep schedule; 22, going to bed and getting up at the same time every day, even on weekends. Also, pay attention to 23 we eat and drink. A light meal, such as a piece of toast or a cup of milk, is perfect. Never go to bed 24 too hungry 24 too full. A large meal within a few hours before bed time might keep us up at night. Avoiding food which contains caffeine and alcohol is necessary, too. Although alcohol might make you feel sleepy at first, it is in fact bad to your sleep later in the night. Last but not least, a restful environment is helpful in having a good sleep as well. Keep your room cool, dark and quiet. It is not difficult to follow the above-mentioned tips. However, if you often have trouble 25, contact your doctors. Hope you have a sweet dream tonight.

She made fliers to remind neighbors and friends to care about those survivors. Earthquakes in Turkey On February 6, 2023, a powerful earthquake, 7.8 magnitude, hit southeast Turkey. The deadly earthquake took away the lives of more than 40,000 people. Tens of thousands were injured, and millions were left homeless. People living in the affected areas were in urgent need of basic necessities like temporary shelters, food, and clean water. Turkey and Taiwan have both been victims of earthquakes. Taiwan can help! You can help! How you can help: ■ Donate money to Red Cross, Doctors Without Borders, World Vision or Tzu Chi Taiwan. These organizations often have experience in effectively providing help for affected areas. ■ Spread the news about the earthquakes on social media. ■ Citizens can also make a donation at convenience stores through self-service machines. ■ Tents, winter clothing, and diapers are needed. Tzu Chi can help you pack the stuff and send it to Turkey. ■ Further contact information in Turkey is as below. Website: https://tzuchi.com.tr/ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/tzuchivakfi

options: What Taiwan and Turkey have in common.
The things that the survivors need the most.

The number of deaths in the earthquake. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 Germany stopped using its last three nuclear power stations on April 15, 2023. In other words, Germany would stop producing any electricity from nuclear power plants. The Emsland, Isar II, and Neckarwestheim II nuclear plants in Germany said goodbye to the public. It is part of the country’s plan to use only renewable energy by 2035. Germany wanted to shut down all its nuclear power stations by 2022; however, the plan was put off due to the Russia-Ukraine war. The public opinions of the shutdown of the nuclear power plants were varied. On the one hand, to those who supported nuclear energy as a clean, zero-carbon source of electricity, the move was seen as a tragedy. Increasing energy prices and energy shortage around Germany were certain to happen. On the other hand, those who feared nuclear accidents believed closing the nuclear power plants was a wise decision and paved the way for Germany’s decision to use renewable energy widely. Germany announced that it would phase out nuclear power after the Fukushima accident in Japan in 2011. The safety of nuclear energy is the major drive that led to the closure of all nuclear power plants. In the past, nuclear power made up one-third of Germany’s energy. Now it’s only 6%. Renewable energy now makes up 44%. By 2030, Germany aims to generate 80 percent of its electricity from renewable energy sources like wind and sun. Some, nevertheless, think that Germany will have to use nuclear power again if it wants to stop using fossil fuels by 2045. Scientists and researchers from MIT wrote an open letter to the German government and said that “this is very disappointing, when a low carbon source of energy such as nuclear was available and could have continued operating for another 40 years...the fast decarbonization should have priority.” The open letter didn’t succeed in keeping the plants working. It is clear that people worldwide are facing the greatest challenge of climate change of all time. There is little time left to deal with the issue of climate change. It seems upset to close down the sources of zero-carbon electric power.
questions.explanation: 下列何項資訊在傳單中沒有提及? ‧ 1 茶匙香草精 (A) 土耳其的地理位置。 ‧鮮奶油 (B) 臺灣和土耳其相同之處。 指示 (C) 倖存者最需要的東西。

questions.q: Which of the following statements is true?
options: Almost all Germans agree to the government’s plan to close the nuclear power plants.
About 6% of energy will be produced by nuclear power plants in Germany after 2035.
The delay of shutting down nuclear power plants in Germany is partly because of the war.
The letter written by scientists from MIT successfully convinced the German government.
questions.explanation: 正解：(C)；來源：112-1-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf
